events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need evaluate sequence check: VFDs & motor controls. Observed field condition: wrong overload class setting. Work actively in progress. Need decide pass/fail/needs_more_info. Since wrong overload class setting is non-compliant and can cause motor damage/fire, decision fail. Risk high or critical? Wrong overload class setting on VFD/motor controls: overload class determines trip curve; wrong setting can cause nuisance tripping or failure to protect against overload, potential motor burnout/fire. In manufacturing floor actively in progress. Risk high. Component condition installed-defective. Work stage in-progress. Percent complete maybe 70? Need estimate overall progress. Value not asked numeric reading, set 0. Sound_source etc default empty string/0. Workable list? Task asks what must happen before work can continue. We can list activity IDs? Not provided. We can use generic IDs like "verify-overload-class", "program-VFD", "functional-test", "inspect-accept". Findings: overload class setting does not match motor FLA/nameplate/application; misconfiguration leaves motor unprotected. Actions: stop work, lockout/tagout, correct overload class per NEC 430 and manufacturer manual, verify motor FLA and service factor, retest, reinspect before energizing. Rationale: overload protection must be set per NEC 430 and manufacturer; wrong class is a sequence violation because configuration/verification must occur before energizing/testing. References: NEC 430.32, 430.37, 430.225? Manufacturer manuals. Need include order? order is for audio/video tasks; not requested. But schema includes order; can leave empty list. event_time 0, rate 0, sound_source "". confidence 0.95. Need valid JSON. Ensure no prose outside JSON.

    "Overload class setting is incorrect for the motor/application.",
    "Misconfiguration can prevent proper overload protection and risks motor damage or fire."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop work and secure the circuit.",
    "Verify motor nameplate FLA, service factor, and insulation class.",
    "Set overload class per NEC 430 and the VFD/motor manufacturer manual.",
    "Perform functional testing and thermal/overload verification before re-energizing.",
    "Reinspect and document settings before accepting the work."
  ],
  "rationale": "Overload protection settings must be verified and correctly configured before the VFD/motor control is energized or placed in service. A wrong overload class setting is a sequence violation because the protection verification step was skipped or performed incorrectly.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 430",
    "VFD and motor manufacturer manuals"
  ]
}
